title: OpenClaw v2026.5.28: Channels, Opus 4.8 & Agent Stability
description: OpenClaw v2026.5.28 hardens 7-platform sessions, adds Claude Opus 4.8, resolves 16+ agent stability issues, ships iOS Pro improvements, and boosts tool performance for B2B SDR teams.
content:
# OpenClaw v2026.5.28: Multi-Channel Security, Claude Opus 4.8 & Agent Stability

OpenClaw released **v2026.5.28** on May 30, 2026 — the most comprehensive stability and security release since v2026.5.0. This version ships 16+ agent runtime fixes, multi-channel session hardening across 7 platforms, Claude Opus 4.8 support, and measurable performance improvements. If your B2B SDR workflows run across WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, iMessage, Matrix, or Microsoft Teams, this update is directly relevant.

Install or upgrade in one command:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template/main/install.sh | bash
```

---

## Multi-Channel Session Safety: 7 Platforms Hardened

The headline change in v2026.5.28 is systematic session hardening across every major chat channel. Session identities are now verified on outbound plugin hooks for all seven platforms.

For B2B teams running [multi-channel sales pipelines](/solutions/multi-channel-sales-pipeline):

- **WhatsApp profile-scoped auth directory resolution** fixes a class of auth failures in multi-account deployments — essential for teams running [WhatsApp sales automation](/solutions/whatsapp-sales-automation) across multiple business numbers
- **Telegram callback page validation** reduces erroneous deliveries in [Telegram lead generation](/solutions/telegram-lead-generation) sequences
- **Discord component ID enforcement** tightened for more reliable notification delivery
- **iMessage and Matrix** receive stability fixes reducing dropped messages in long-running campaigns
- **Microsoft Teams** added to the hardened session identity set

The practical impact: fewer phantom delivery failures, less morning queue backlog from overnight automation, and stronger compliance posture for enterprise accounts.

---

## Claude Opus 4.8 + Provider Expansion

v2026.5.28 adds Claude Opus 4.8 to the supported model list alongside:

| Provider | Addition |
|----------|----------|
| Anthropic | Claude Opus 4.8 |
| Fal | Krea image generation schemas |
| NVIDIA | Full model catalog |
| MiniMax | Streaming music generation |
| ClawPDF | Encrypted PDF extraction |
| GitHub | Copilot agent runtime support |

For [AI SDR teams in B2B export](/solutions/ai-sdr-for-b2b-export) and [manufacturing](/solutions/ai-sales-agent-for-manufacturing), Claude Opus 4.8's improved reasoning makes it the strongest option for complex prospect research and multi-step qualification flows. Route demanding tasks to Opus 4.8 while keeping lighter qualification work on faster, cost-optimal models.

Encrypted PDF extraction via ClawPDF enables automated ingestion of password-protected RFQs and procurement documents — a frequent pain point in manufacturing-sector outreach.

---

## Agent & Codex Runtime: 16+ Stability Fixes

This release's stability backbone targets the failure modes that cause the most operational pain in production SDR automation:

| Fix | Operational Impact |
|-----|-------------------|
| Subagent workspace separation | No cross-workspace bleed in multi-team deploys |
| Prompt-local hook context | Hooks behave predictably across concurrent sequences |
| Timeout-based session lock release | Hung sessions no longer block the next scheduled task |
| Stale restart continuations prevented | Agents resume correctly after crash or container restart |
| Codex helper failure isolation | One failing helper doesn't tear down the whole runtime |
| Tool argument repair (quoted/escaped inputs) | Fewer malformed calls to CRM and enrichment APIs |

The session lock release fix is particularly high-value for teams running overnight automation: stale locks now expire automatically instead of requiring manual intervention before the morning queue can proceed.

---

## Performance: Session-Level Tool Catalog Reuse

v2026.5.28 ships targeted performance improvements:

- **Install-record caching** — repeated reinstalls in CI/CD pipelines skip redundant work
- **Config parsing optimization** — faster startup for agents with large configuration trees
- **Tool catalog reuse** — the catalog is now fetched once per session instead of per-call; CRM lookup and lead enrichment tools respond faster on the second and subsequent invocations
- **Session store improvements** — reduced I/O on high-frequency session reads

For [manufacturing sector AI sales agents](/solutions/ai-sales-agent-for-manufacturing) running dozens of simultaneous enrichment calls, session-level catalog reuse is the change most likely to show up as measurable latency reduction.

---

## Auth & CLI: Security Hygiene Upgrades

- **Workspace dotenv credentials no longer auto-loaded** — prevents accidental `.env` file contents from bleeding into agent context
- **Bounded OAuth/token lifetimes** — tokens expire on schedule, required for SOC 2 and enterprise compliance programs
- **Malformed numeric inputs rejected** — closes a class of injection via crafted numeric arguments
- **Clearer auth health labels** — operators can diagnose auth failures without parsing raw logs

---

## iOS Pro Refresh + Mobile Push

The iOS client receives a significant upgrade: Pro Command, Chat, Agents, and Settings are fully integrated in the refreshed interface. **Hosted push relay defaults** are enabled out of the box — mobile sales reps now receive agent notifications without manual relay server configuration. Realtime Talk playback enables voice-enabled outreach review on mobile.

---

## Upgrade Checklist for v2026.5.28

Before rolling out to production outreach sequences:

- [ ] **WhatsApp**: Review auth directory paths — profile-scoped resolution may change credential lookup location
- [ ] **OAuth-dependent workflows**: Set explicit token rotation logic before upgrading
- [ ] **Workspace dotenv**: Audit `.env` files in workspace directories — they will no longer auto-load
- [ ] **Model configs**: Explicitly configure Claude Opus 4.8 if you want to use it

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is v2026.5.28 a breaking change?**
Not by default for most deployments. The workspace dotenv and OAuth token lifetime changes are behavioral — test in a staging environment before rolling to production.

**Does Claude Opus 4.8 cost more than Opus 4.6?**
OpenClaw passes through Anthropic's pricing. For most SDR workloads, Sonnet 4.6 remains cost-optimal. Reserve Opus 4.8 for your highest-value research tasks.

**How does session lock release affect overnight automation?**
Previously, a hung session blocked subsequent scheduled tasks until manual intervention. The timeout-based release means stale locks expire automatically, eliminating morning queue backlog.

**Which channel had the most significant fixes?**
WhatsApp (profile-scoped auth), Telegram (callback validation), and Microsoft Teams (new to hardened set) saw the largest changes. All seven platforms received session identity verification.

---
